steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence in the passage. The sentence describes Yung Wing writing about an imaginary trip to New York as a child — and the very next sentence states a general lesson: imagination sometimes foreshadows what is most on our minds.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It indicates Yung's unwillingness to distinguish between reality and fantasy as a child.: The passage never suggests Yung is confused about what was real. ✗
  • Choice B — It foreshadows Yung's future difficulties in publishing his writings.: Nothing in the passage mentions publishing. ✗
  • Choice C — It describes an event in Yung's life that exemplifies a phenomenon.: The childhood writing episode is the specific example that illustrates the broader claim made in the next sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — It illustrates the sense of adventure that Yung developed as a child.: The passage draws no such theme; the sentence serves a structural, not characterizing, role. ✗
C is correct.
